prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
At last, the final line is drawn,
The final burden laid to rest.
The weary clock has greeted dawn,
And proved the doing was the test.

A hollow, clean and quiet space,
Where once the frantic details swirled.
The finished thing has found its place,
A small new corner of the world.

Now stillness settles, deep and sweet,
A lightness in the hands and head.
The long endeavor is complete,
And I am empty, and well-fed.
